source_pr.title: ttl: fix data race in TTL manager and test
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: YangKeao <yangkeao@chunibyo.icu>

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #40944

Problem Summary:

Fix the data race to read and initialize the TTL job manager.

### What is changed and how it works?

Use the atomic pointer to store the TTL job manager.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

test.name: ttl: fix data race in TTL manager and test

symptoms: Race detector reports a data race between TTL job manager initialization and reads (Domain.StartTTLJobManager vs Domain.TTLJobManager).
failure_signature: DATA RACE on Domain.ttlJobManager (concurrent Store/Load without synchronization)
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness was caused by a real data race: the TTL job manager pointer on Domain could be initialized in StartTTLJobManager while other goroutines read it via TTLJobManager() without synchronization. This PR fixes the race by storing the JobManager in an atomic.Pointer and using Store/Load for safe concurrent access.
fix_pattern: Replace the raw Domain.ttlJobManager pointer with atomic.Pointer and use Store/Load to synchronize concurrent initialization and reads.

evidence: evidence: ttl: fix data race in TTL manager and test
